frozen_components: LLM backbone during latent-text alignment stage
Evaluator LLM used for process reward computation
architecture.summary: Discrete Latent Reasoning (DLR) converts continuous latent reasoning states into explicit discrete tokens via a render-based compression pipeline. CoT text is rendered to images, encoded by a DeepSeek-OCR2 visual compressor, and vector-quantized into a learned codebook. These discrete latent tokens are then integrated into an LLM's vocabulary through lightweight projectors, enabling standard autoregressive modeling over both natural language and latent tokens. The system trains in three stages: latent-text alignment (synchronizing embeddings), latent SFT (teaching latent trajectory generation), and latent RL (optimizing via process and outcome rewards). An auxiliary decoder reconstructs text from latent trajectories for interpretability.
trained_components: DeepSeek-OCR2 latent codebook (vector quantization + curriculum learning)
Input/output projectors mapping codebook vectors to LLM embedding space
LLM backbone and projectors during SFT and RL stages
Auxiliary OCR decoder for latent-to-text reconstruction

biblio.title: Why Struggle with Continuous Latents? Interpretable Discrete Latent Reasoning via Rendered Compression

claims.statement: DLR achieves up to 20× compression compared to explicit chain-of-thought while maintaining or improving reasoning accuracy
On GSM8K-Aug with Qwen3-4B, DLR (Full) achieves 62.2% accuracy, outperforming the second-best latent baseline CoLaR (40.0%) by 22.2 percentage points
On MATH-500 with Qwen3-VL-8B, DLR (Full) achieves 54.0% accuracy, a 9.4 point gain over DLR (SFT), demonstrating consistent scaling benefit from latent RL
DLR maintains compressed reasoning length around 6.5 tokens on GSM8K-Aug (Qwen3-4B), compared to 119.8 tokens for explicit CoT-SFT and 32.0 tokens for rendered-CoT baseline RoT
Optimal latent codebook vocabulary size is 4096 tokens (κ=4096), balancing representation capacity and generalization; larger vocabularies (κ=8192) degrade performance due to under-utilization

The auxiliary OCR decoder translates learned latent trajectories into interpretable step-by-step reasoning chains; minor hallucinations (e.g., name or unit substitutions) do not disrupt underlying causal reasoning logic
Removing the latent-text alignment stage (Stage 1) causes major performance drop; forcing latent tokens to co-train with the backbone from scratch fails to ground them semantically

Omitting the Process Alignment Reward during latent RL strictly degrades accuracy across all four grade-school benchmarks; process-level verification is critical for semantic coherence of latent trajectories
